new_papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
new_papers.authors: Jimenez, Yang, Wettig, Yao, Pei, Press, Narasimhan
new_papers.summary: Introduces SWE-bench: 2,294 real GitHub issues across 12 Python repos. The FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS regression-test verifier is the load-bearing methodological detail that made repo-level coding eval tractable. SWE-bench Verified (OpenAI Aug 2024) is the 500-task human-validated subset that became the de-facto leaderboard target.

new_papers.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
new_papers.authors: Mialon, Fourrier, Wolf, Lavril, LeCun, et al. (HuggingFace + Meta)
new_papers.summary: 466 tool-use questions (300 leaderboard / 166 dev) stratified into Levels 1/2/3. Headline gap: human 92% vs GPT-4 + plugins 15%. Gated-leaderboard methodology (gold answers held server-side) is the contamination-defense design point.

new_papers.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
new_papers.authors: Xie, Zhang, Chen, Lou, Liu, Hu, Xing, Hu, Lin, Liu, Yu
new_papers.summary: 369 computer-use tasks across Ubuntu/Windows/macOS with per-task executable evaluator scripts checking OS end-state. Headline gap at launch: humans 72% vs GPT-4V 12%. Failure mode: GUI grounding bottleneck.

new_papers.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
new_papers.authors: Yao, Zhao, Wang, Schick, Lewis, Yih, Lecun, Yu (Sierra Research)
new_papers.summary: Customer-service agentic benchmark with simulated-user + policy compliance + DB-end-state verifier. Introduces pass^k metric (all k attempts must succeed; the production-reliability dual of pass@k). GPT-4o: <50% pass@1, <25% pass^8 on retail.

new_papers.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
new_papers.authors: Liang, Bommasani, Lee, Tsipras, Soylu, Yasunaga, Zhang, Narayanan, Wu, et al. (Stanford CRFM)
new_papers.summary: HELM: 7-metric x 16-scenario evaluation framework. The structural argument that benchmarks should ship metric bundles (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency), not single metrics. Increased coverage from 17.9% to 96% of core scenarios across 30 LMs at launch.

new_papers.title: Benchmarking LLMs Under Data Contamination: From Static to Dynamic Evaluation
new_papers.summary: Comprehensive survey of contamination problem and mitigation strategies. Establishes the static-vs-dynamic-evaluation taxonomy. Identifies key open problem: lack of standardized criteria for evaluating dynamic benchmarks.

new_papers.title: EleutherAI Language Model Evaluation Harness
new_papers.authors: Gao, Tow, Abbasi, Biderman, Black, et al. (EleutherAI)
new_papers.summary: Open-source eval infrastructure backing HF Open LLM Leaderboard. 60+ benchmarks reduced to three request-type primitives (loglikelihood / loglikelihood_rolling / generate_until). Adopted by NVIDIA, Cohere, BigScience, Mosaic ML internally; the de-facto standard for open-model evaluation.

new_papers.title: Are We Done with MMLU?
new_papers.summary: Verifier-audit of MMLU. Re-annotates 5,700 questions across 57 subjects; finds 6.49% aggregate error rate; per-subset rates up to 57% (Virology). Documents leaderboard-rank flips when corrected.

new_papers.title: MathArena: Evaluating LLMs on Uncontaminated Math Competitions
new_papers.summary: Dynamic benchmark using post-cutoff competition problems (CMIMC 2025, IMO 2025, etc.) to defeat training-data contamination. Evaluates models on problems immediately upon release. >50 models, 7 competitions, 162 problems as of 2025.

new_papers.title: Cybench: A Framework for Evaluating Cybersecurity Capabilities and Risks of Language Models
new_papers.summary: 40 professional Capture-the-Flag cybersecurity tasks across 4 CTF competitions. Used by US AISI / UK AISI for pre-deployment dangerous-capability evals. Top frontier models (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, o1-preview, Claude 3 Opus) solve tasks taking human teams up to 11 minutes.

new_papers.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
new_papers.summary: 8-environment agentic benchmark (OS, DB, KG, web, etc.). Headline finding: significant capability gap between top commercial LLMs and open-source <70B alternatives. Failure mode: poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, instruction following.

new_papers.title: ARC-AGI-2 (and ARC Prize 2025 Technical Report)
new_papers.authors: Chollet et al. (ARC Prize Foundation); Chapman et al. for tech report
new_papers.summary: Successor to ARC-AGI-1 (which was effectively solved by o3 at >85% in late 2024). ARC-AGI-2 launches early 2025; top commercial model ~37.6%, refinement-loop systems ~54%, human baseline ~95%. Tests inductive grid-puzzle generalization with minimal world knowledge.
